spec_match.rationale: Locale picker + per-getter cell showing getter value, method value, and equivalence — touches all seven deprecated getters.

interactive.rationale: Real <select> drives live re-render with seven cards; calls both API forms live in the browser.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card promises 'every getter and replacement called side-by-side' — delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: When values are identical the card shows 'same value', otherwise '(equivalent)' — but this doesn't account for object-shape differences (e.g. weekInfo returns an object, so JSON.stringify of getter vs method may differ in key order). No demo of what happens if Intl.Locale.prototype is missing one of the methods (older engines).

references.rationale: See also has no chromestatus.com link, no spec link. Rule #3 regression.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables, --accent-blue for the equivalence sub-text, all colour pairs hit AA.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus.com link
openQuestions.detail: Rule #3: https://chromestatus.com/feature/5148228059398144 must be in see-also. Currently sibling-only.

openQuestions.title: Show structural equality, not string equality
openQuestions.detail: JSON.stringify is order-sensitive on plain objects. Use a deep-equal check (Set comparison for arrays, structural deep-equal for objects) so the 'same value' verdict is reliable.

openQuestions.title: Add a per-method probe with versioning
openQuestions.detail: Each method has a different ship date (getCalendars vs getTextInfo). Annotate each card with the Chrome version that introduced the method so visitors can audit their browser-min targets.

summary: Useful per-getter dashboard with live API calls. Missing the chromestatus reference link and uses JSON.stringify equality, which can give spurious 'not equivalent' verdicts on object key-order drift.
